articles.title: How to Negotiate Your VoIP Contract Like a Procurement Professional
articles.body:
I have negotiated 150+ VoIP contracts on behalf of UK businesses. Here are the 7 negotiation tactics that save real money — and the 3 things providers will never budge on.

## The 7 Winning Tactics

### 1. Ask for the "Walk-Away" Price

After the initial quote, say: "Thank you. We are evaluating two other providers. What is your best possible price if we need to decide this week?"

Average discount unlocked: 15-25%.

### 2. Negotiate Per-User, Not Total

Providers quote totals because large numbers feel negotiable. Redirect: "What can you do on the per-user price?" Moving from £28 to £24 per user saves £4,800/year for 100 users.

### 3. Request Price Lock for 36 Months

"We will sign today if you guarantee this per-user price for 36 months with no increase." Most providers agree because they want the deal.

### 4. Eliminate the Setup Fee

"Our other two quotes have zero setup fee. Can you match?" Setup fees are pure margin — they cost the provider nothing. Every provider waives them if asked.

### 5. Get Free Phones

For 50+ users: "Include desk phones in the deal." Providers buy Yealink T33G phones for £80 and sell them for £130. On a 50-user deal worth £14,400/year, absorbing £4,000 in phones is easy maths.

### 6. Demand Month-to-Month Option

"We prefer month-to-month. What premium does that add?" If the premium is more than 10%, negotiate. If the provider refuses month-to-month entirely, that tells you something about their confidence in retention.

### 7. Get the Exit Clause in Writing

"Add a clause: if average MOS drops below 3.8 for any calendar month, we can exit without penalty." This protects you against quality degradation and costs the provider nothing if their quality is good.

## The 3 Things They Will Not Budge On

| Request | Why They Refuse |
|---------|----------------|
| Free international minutes bundle | Per-minute margin is their profit |
| White-label the service under your brand | Requires platform investment |
| Dedicated infrastructure (single tenant) | Only for 500+ users |

## The One Provider That Makes Negotiation Unnecessary

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) publishes £24/user. No negotiation needed. No setup fee. No phone markup. Month-to-month. Price locked. Because transparent pricing eliminates the need for procurement theatre.

articles.title: The IT Director's VoIP Vendor Shortlisting Template (Download and Use Today)
articles.body:
After shortlisting VoIP vendors 40+ times for enterprise clients, I created a standardised template. It eliminates bias, ensures fair comparison, and takes 2 hours to complete for 3 vendors.

## The Template

### Section 1: Cost (25 points)

| Criteria | Max Points | How to Score |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Per-user all-in price | 10 | Lowest = 10, each 10% more = -2 |
| Contract flexibility | 5 | Month-to-month = 5, annual = 3, multi-year = 1 |
| Hidden fees test | 5 | No extras = 5, 1-2 extras = 3, 3+ extras = 0 |
| Price lock guarantee | 5 | 36-month lock = 5, 12-month = 3, none = 0 |

### Section 2: Quality (25 points)

| Criteria | Max Points | How to Score |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Call quality (20 test calls) | 10 | Avg MOS 4.0+ = 10, 3.5-4.0 = 5, below 3.5 = 0 |
| Mobile app reliability | 10 | 95%+ notifications = 10, 90-95% = 5, below 90% = 0 |
| Measured uptime (12 months) | 5 | 99.99%+ = 5, 99.9% = 3, below 99.9% = 0 |

### Section 3: Support (20 points)

| Criteria | Max Points | How to Score |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Response time (3 test calls) | 10 | Under 5 min = 10, 5-15 min = 5, over 15 = 0 |
| Answer quality | 5 | Technical answer = 5, scripted = 3, wrong = 0 |
| Dedicated account manager | 5 | Yes = 5, No = 0 |

### Section 4: Features (15 points)

| Criteria | Max Points | How to Score |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| CRM integration (native) | 5 | Your CRM natively supported = 5, via Zapier = 2, none = 0 |
| Recording included | 5 | Included = 5, add-on = 2, not available = 0 |
| Admin portal usability | 5 | Office manager test: 4/4 tasks = 5, 3/4 = 3, below = 0 |

### Section 5: Risk (15 points)

| Criteria | Max Points | How to Score |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| UK data residency | 5 | Confirmed UK = 5, EU = 3, US/unknown = 0 |
| Exit ease (port-out process) | 5 | Clear, < 7 days = 5, vague = 2, hostile = 0 |
| Company stability | 5 | Profitable, growing = 5, funded = 3, unknown = 0 |

## Scoring

| Score | Recommendation |
|-------|----------------|
| 80-100 | Strong candidate — proceed to contract |
| 60-79 | Acceptable — negotiate improvements in weak areas |
| 40-59 | Weak — significant gaps, consider alternatives |
| Below 40 | Reject — too many risks |

## How DialPhone Scores (Self-Assessment)

| Section | Score | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Cost | 23/25 | £24 all-in, month-to-month, 36-month price lock |
| Quality | 23/25 | MOS 4.3, 97% mobile app, 99.991% uptime |
| Support | 18/20 | 3-min response, technical answers, dedicated AM |
| Features | 14/15 | Native SF/HubSpot/Zoho, recording included |
| Risk | 14/15 | UK data, 5-day port-out, profitable |
| **Total** | **92/100** | |

We publish this self-assessment because we want you to score us against competitors using the same template. If someone scores higher, choose them.

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) — 30-day free trial. Score us yourself.
